failure_modes: Buying at a low cone percentile while the variance risk premium is positive: you systematically overpay; low IV-vs-RV-history does not imply positive long-vol expectancy (Coval-Shumway / Broadie-Chernov-Johannes evidence)

Treating low percentile as a mean-reversion buy signal when vol is persistent: in a calm regime low IV begets low realized vol, so the long-gamma 'spike up' never materializes within the holding period and theta bleeds the position
Cheap-for-a-reason regimes: structured-product flow (Bennett vicious circle) holds IV down in recoveries, or genuinely lower fundamental risk (Ch.15) — the cone reads 'cheap' but there is no edge

Over-trusting Hodges-Tompkins to manufacture statistical confidence from short history: the ~3% bias fix does not rescue a thin sample or a data-mined percentile threshold, and overlapping windows still leave estimates highly autocorrelated
Single-name cone in isolation: a low percentile on the stock may be no cheaper than the index after VRP — Sinclair's index-spread benchmark shows the apparent edge can vanish on a relative basis

Look-ahead / regime drift in the cone itself: a 2-4 year cone built across a structural vol regime change misplaces today's percentile (the cap and recent-vs-analog-period guidance in Bennett A.2 address this only partially)

Asymmetry neglect: the rule is symmetric in the candidate but Sinclair's evidence is asymmetric — the cone is a sound SELL filter and a weak BUY filter; applying it equally both ways inherits the weaker edge on the long side
lenses.required_modifications: Recast from a long-vol BUY trigger to a context-and-sizing tool that is sell-biased and VRP-aware: (1) Replace 'buy vol when IV at low cone percentile' with 'enter long vol only when low IV percentile COINCIDES with a regime-conditioned RV forecast ABOVE current IV net of the typical variance risk premium for that underlying/tenor' — make VRP an explicit subtraction, not an omission. (2) Keep the cone as Sinclair intends: primarily a richness filter for SELLING (high percentile) and a distributional-context/position-sizing input, not a standalone entry signal. (3) Make regime conditioning and the index IV/RV-spread benchmark MANDATORY pre-conditions, not optional caveats — reject low-percentile setups attributable to structured-product flow (Bennett 3.4) or to genuinely lower fundamentals (Ch.15). (4) Retain Hodges-Tompkins (Eq 4.16) only as a sampling-bias correction with the honest caveat that it is a few-percent fix, not a source of edge, and does not cure small-sample or regime-shift problems. (5) Match the cone/HV tenor to the implied tenor per Bennett A.2 but cap windows near the ~8-month mean-reversion horizon and, after a rare vol spike, prefer an analog historical period over the most recent window. (6) Require an explicit catalyst/why-is-it-cheap thesis before any low-percentile long-vol entry, per Sinclair's 'cheap for a reason' rule.

lenses.key_reasoning:
The candidate is a GATE, not a strategy: it only authorizes a long-vol event trade when a defensible forecast move comfortably exceeds the market's implied jump, after stripping diffusive vol and macro term-structure slope. As a gate it is robust precisely because it inverts the naive "buy vol over earnings" trade that the counterclaims attack. The core mechanism is an identity, not a curve fit: time-weighted variance is additive (Bennett 6.4, trading_vol.txt L7150-7166), so front-month-after-event IV^2 * T = sigma_jump^2 + sigma_diffusive^2 * (T-1), and forward vol between the two near expiries isolates diffusion. E|R| = sqrt(2/pi)*sigma_E is the mean absolute deviation of a normal (Bennett, trading_vol.txt L9315), so it is a clean translation of jump vol into an implied expected move. None of this is overfit — it has no estimated parameters beyond the IVs you observe plus your own move forecast. The index-term-structure adjustment (Bennett 6.4, L7188-7232: single-stock TS = index/macro TS + reporting jump) is a genuine de-biasing step that prevents an upward-sloping macro curve from making the jump look richer than it is.

REGIMES WHERE IT WORKS: (1) Genuine uncertainty / analyst disagreement — Sinclair's own empirical test (sinclair.txt L4179-4239, Table 5.1) found the ONLY fundamental factor that predicted long-straddle profitability was high analyst-estimate dispersion (quartile P/L 3/10/14/48). Dispersion is the regime where the market under-prices the jump relative to the true distribution. (2) Low/negative single-stock variance-risk-premium windows where IV has not yet caught up to a building catalyst. (3) Names with a structurally fat-tailed earnings move distribution (the long straddle's edge is its positive skew: Sinclair's test showed only 43% win rate but win/loss size 1.70 and skewness 5.4, kurtosis 76.6 — sinclair.txt L4142-4145). The edge lives in the tail, so it requires many independent events to realize.

REGIMES WHERE IT FAILS: (1) Hyped/telegraphed catalysts — the implied jump already embeds the move (Sinclair Ch.14), so even a large forecast move fails the "comfortably exceeds" test. (2) Post-memorable-jump availability bias — after a dramatic recent move the implied jump is extrapolated too high (Sinclair Ch.10, the Google 16% implied vs 7.7% avg realized example, sinclair.txt L9844-9860), so the gate should reject, and if a naive constant-vol estimate is used it OVERSTATES the jump and the trade bleeds. (3) Flat-or-inverted single-stock term structure: if front-month IV is not even above the second month, the market prices no event and a held straddle is pure theta bleed — this is the candidate's own stated kill switch and it is correct.

The candidate's verdict is holds-with-caveats rather than holds because (a) it inherits two estimation hazards from its own sources — the diffusive-vol input is biased if you assume constant vol, since post-event diffusive vol is typically ~3/4 of pre-event (Bennett 6.4, trading_vol.txt L7160-7162), which inflates the residual you attribute to the jump; and (b) the positive variance risk premium (the user's own NIFTY/single-stock VRP is positive ~75% of days per the memory profile and forecast-vs-implied candidate) is a persistent headwind that the gate reduces but does not eliminate — you are buying into a structurally rich surface.
failure_modes: Theta/carry bleed in the no-event regime: if front-month IV is not above second-month (flat or inverted single-stock TS), the market prices no jump and a held straddle is pure time decay. This is the dominant slow-bleed failure and is the gate's own kill switch.

Persistent positive VRP headwind: single-stock and index IV is structurally above realized; you are buying a rich surface, so even a correct directional-magnitude forecast can lose if the move lands below the (already elevated) implied jump. The 43% win rate means long droughts are normal — under-sizing or impatience kills the strategy before the tail pays.

Diffusive-vol estimation bias: using a constant-vol or naive HV diffusive input overstates sigma_jump because post-event diffusion is ~3/4 of pre-event (Bennett). This makes the implied jump look smaller than it is, so the gate FALSELY passes and you overpay. Must use the forward-vol-between-expiries estimate, and ideally shrink it for the post-event regime.
Telegraphed/hyped catalyst (Sinclair Ch.14): the move is already in the implied jump; the forecast rarely 'comfortably exceeds' it, and if you fudge the forecast you buy into a fully-priced event.

Availability bias after a memorable jump (Sinclair Ch.10): implied jump is over-extrapolated; if you instead trust a naive long-history forecast you may think it is rich (a SHORT signal), but if your forecast itself is anchored on the recent dramatic move you over-pay long. Either way the recent-move anchor corrupts both the implied and the forecast inputs.

Wide bid-offer / illiquidity on NSE single-stock F&O: implied-jump edges are small and near-dated; spreads on less-liquid single stocks can exceed the edge (Bennett notes jumps are only tradeable on near-dated, active names). On NSE only the most liquid F&O names qualify.
Pin/path risk and assignment: holding a straddle through expiry on a name that pins near the strike (Bennett L1911) and the leg-management cost on a small retail account erode the gross edge.

Overfitting the gate threshold: 'comfortably exceeds' is a free parameter. Tuning the margin on historical earnings to maximize backtest P/L will curve-fit; the margin must be set ex ante and held constant.

Tail-dependence / small-sample blow-up: the edge is in rare large moves (kurtosis 76.6). A retail trader running few events per quarter on NSE may go an entire sample without realizing the tail, making a true edge look refuted and tempting premature abandonment.
lenses.required_modifications: ["REGIME FILTER 1 (term-structure gate): require front-month (post-event) ATM IV strictly above second-month, AND require the index-adjusted single-stock term structure to show a positive reporting-date residual after subtracting NIFTY/sector index TS. No positive residual => no trade. This strips the macro-slope false positive (Bennett Fig 96-97).", "REGIME FILTER 2 (dispersion / uncertainty gate): only take longs where there is genuine forecast disagreement (wide analyst-estimate dispersion or a real binary catalyst — regulatory ruling, guidance reset). Sinclair's data show this is the only fundamental that predicts long-straddle profitability. Skip telegraphed, consensus, low-dispersion events (Sinclair Ch.14).", "DIFFUSIVE-VOL CORRECTION: estimate diffusive vol from forward vol between the two near expiries, NOT from constant/historical vol, and shrink the post-event diffusive leg toward ~3/4 of pre-event (Bennett L7160-7162) so you do not understate the implied jump and overpay.", "FORECAST DISCIPLINE: build the move forecast from the FULL history of that name's earnings moves (not the most recent dramatic one) to neutralize availability bias (Sinclair Ch.10 Google example). Require forecast E|R| to exceed implied jump by a fixed, pre-registered margin (e.g. >=25-30%) set ex ante to avoid curve-fitting the threshold.", "VRP HAIRCUT: because Indian VRP is positive (~75% of days), add a fixed implied-vol premium haircut to the implied jump before comparing to your forecast, so the gate clears only when the edge survives the structural richness, not just a point estimate.", "TIMING: separate the two distinct edges. If you want the pre-event IV-ramp edge (Sinclair's profitable variant), enter ~10 days out and EXIT the day before the announcement (sell into the IV peak, avoid the crush). Only HOLD THROUGH the event when the gate shows a large, under-priced jump and your forecast move clears the margin with room.", "SIZING: small fixed-fraction per event (e.g. <=0.5-1% of capital), many independent events, never concentrate — the payoff is positive-skew/low-win-rate (43% win, 1.70 win/loss, kurtosis 76.6), so survival to the tail requires diversification across names and quarters and tolerance for losing streaks. Pre-commit to a minimum sample (e.g. 30-50 events) before judging the edge.", "LIQUIDITY/COST FILTER: restrict to the most liquid NSE F&O single stocks and near-dated expiries; reject any name where round-trip bid-offer plus hedging cost consumes more than ~a third of the gross expected edge. Model NSE fees/STT explicitly (self-sourced data, custom QC fee model) since the per-event edge is thin.", "EXPRESSION ON NSE: since India VIX is untradeable and QC lacks NSE options data, implement as a self-sourced option-data screen feeding stock/index straddles or the relative-value calendar (long expiry-just-after, short expiry-just-before) that Bennett describes — the calendar isolates the jump and reduces the diffusive/VRP bleed versus an outright straddle."]

lenses.key_reasoning:
The core principle survives NSE reality and is actually REINFORCED by Indian frictions, but the candidate must be recast from "rolling tail hedge" to "event-conditioned convexity," because NSE-specific costs make a continuously-rolled put hedge even more punishing than in the US.

1) The "expensive, leaky, historically unprofitable standalone" thesis transfers. AQR's work (the canonical source) confirms a 5%-OTM S&P put rolled monthly has been a money-loser even including the 1987 crash, precisely because buying optionality pays the variance + equity-risk premium. India runs a positive VRP too (IV > subsequent RV ~74.9% of days, mean +1.2 vol pts), so a rolling long-put hedge bleeds the premium on the buy side. The India VRP is SMALLER than the US (~+1.2 vol pts vs the US ~4.1-pt VIX-RV gap), which cuts two ways: each individual put is less egregiously overpriced than US puts (so the per-unit bleed is somewhat lower), but the protection is also thinner and the premium inverts ~25% of the time, so the hedge is unreliable exactly when you'd want it cheap.

2) NSE frictions make a rolling tail hedge WORSE than US, not better. The flat Rs 20/order (Rs 40 round-trip) brokerage is regressive on cheap tails: a far-OTM NIFTY put at Rs 2/unit costs ~30%+ of premium round-trip in fixed costs alone, vs ~0.8% for a liquid ATM. So the cheapest, most "convex" tail strikes are exactly the ones the cost stack destroys. Far-OTM NIFTY puts also carry wide spreads (e.g. Rs 2 bid / Rs 3.5 ask ≈ 40% entry slippage) and liquidity evaporates after ~2PM near expiry. A naive monthly far-OTM put ladder can lose 40-70%/yr to bid/ask + brokerage + theta before any VRP bleed — well above the candidate's 1-2% cost cap, meaning the cap forces you to far-OTM strikes that are precisely the illiquid, fixed-cost-dominated ones. This is the central tension to resolve.

3) The candidate's "demand a mispricing / buy convexity when calm is just breaking, not at peak fear" rule maps cleanly onto India's event calendar, and the fact base lets us sharpen it into a hard rule: long puts THROUGH Budget and RBI policy reliably LOSE (India VIX fell on all 15 budget days 2011-2025, avg >9%; RBI is a milder, mixed crush) — these are short-vol events, not hedge entries. The one event that genuinely pays long-vol through it is a contested general ELECTION: June 4, 2024 saw India VIX +27.7% to 26.74 (intraday ~31) while NIFTY fell ~6%/-1,379 pts — confirmed. So the timing rule for India is: never hold a tail hedge into Budget/RBI; the only calendar-justified long-gamma entry is into a genuinely uncertain national election where IV is still cheap (e.g. 2024 entered from a VIX ~8-mid-teens).

4) Instrument & venue constraints force one specific expression. India VIX is untradable (futures dead since ~2016; no options/ETF/ETN ever listed), so a tail hedge MUST be built from NIFTY index puts directly — there is no VIX-call alternative. Index options are cash-settled (good: no physical-delivery trap), whereas single-stock options are physically settled with only a narrow ~3-strike DNE band — so single names are a non-starter for a clean index tail hedge anyway, and their skews are shallower/VRP smaller, so the index is both the cleaner and the only practical hedge instrument. NIFTY is the only NSE index with WEEKLY expiry (now Tuesday since Sep-1-2025); BANKNIFTY/FINNIFTY/MIDCPNIFTY are monthly-only. Lot/notional: NIFTY lot 65 (re-baselined down from 75 for Jan-2026), ~Rs 16-17 lakh notional — coarse sizing for a retail book, so a sub-2% cost cap on a small portfolio may buy only a fraction of one lot's worth of protection, a real sizing problem.

5) QC path is severely constrained but workable for research-only. QC has NO native NSE options/F&O data and its Zerodha/Samco live plugins are equity/commodity-only (no F&O routing). So you cannot backtest or live-execute this in LEAN's native options stack. The only honest paths are: (a) custom NSE option data + self-computed Greeks (QuantLib/py_vollib) + a custom FeeModel encoding Rs 20/order + 0.10% sell-side STT + exchange/GST/stamp, used for SIGNAL/research only, with execution via Kite Connect outside LEAN; or (b) prototype the *logic* on QC-native US SPX/VIX options (everything works out-of-the-box) and accept it as a proxy, knowing the India VRP is smaller and the cost stack different. There is no turnkey QC route.

Net: the principle holds (don't run a perpetual put hedge; cap cost; demand mispricing; time entries to breaking calm), but in India it must become an EVENT-CONDITIONED, election-only convexity buy on NIFTY index puts, sized within a strict cap that the fixed-brokerage + spread reality makes hard to honor — hence holds-with-caveats.
failure_modes: Cost-cap vs liquidity trap: honoring the 1-2%/yr cost cap forces you to far-OTM NIFTY strikes — precisely the ones where flat Rs 40 brokerage is ~30%+ of premium and bid/ask is ~40% wide, so realized cost blows past the cap and convexity is eaten on entry/exit before any tail event.

Holding a hedge THROUGH the wrong event: buying/holding NIFTY puts into Union Budget (VIX fell all 15/15 days 2011-2025, avg >9%) or RBI policy guarantees IV-crush bleed. Only a genuinely contested national election (2024-style) has paid long vol through the event in India.

Lot-size indivisibility: NIFTY lot 65 at ~Rs 16-17 lakh notional means a small retail book under a strict cost cap can afford only a fraction of one lot of protection, leaving the hedge either uneconomic or too coarse to size precisely.

Liquidity timing: OTM NIFTY put liquidity evaporates after ~2PM and into weekly (Tuesday) expiry — a tail event triggering intraday can leave you unable to monetize the put at fair value, or rolling at punitive spreads.

VRP inversion / thin protection: India's VRP is smaller than the US and inverts ~25% of the time, so the hedge is least cheap exactly going into shocks; you can pay up for puts that still under-deliver relative to a US-sized premium.

No QC backtest fidelity: with no native NSE options data and equity-only Zerodha/Samco plugins, any QC backtest is either on self-built custom data (no validated Greeks/fills) or a US SPX/VIX proxy whose VRP and cost stack differ — so live India P&L can diverge materially from the modeled result.

Physical-settlement trap if drifting to single names: substituting illiquid single-stock puts for the index hedge risks compulsory physical delivery (DNE only covers ~3 strikes), turning a hedge into an unwanted equity position.
lenses.required_modifications: Recast from "perpetual rolling NIFTY-put tail hedge" to "event-conditioned NIFTY-put convexity, election-only." Concrete NSE expression: (1) Instrument: NIFTY 50 index puts only (cash-settled; the only NSE index with weekly Tuesday expiry and the only liquid, untradable-VIX-substitute hedge). Avoid single-stock puts (physical settlement, narrow DNE, shallower skew/smaller VRP) and avoid BANKNIFTY/FINNIFTY/MIDCPNIFTY for tail use (monthly-only). (2) Strike/leg: use a near-OTM-to-5%-OTM put SPREAD (long ~3-5% OTM, short ~8-12% OTM) rather than a lone far-OTM put — the short leg recovers premium and, crucially, sidesteps the regressive Rs 40 fixed brokerage + ~40% spread that destroys lone far-OTM tails; size in whole NIFTY lots (65) and acknowledge ~Rs 16-17 lakh notional granularity. (3) Maturity: match feared-decline duration — for an election, use a monthly expiry that brackets result day, NOT a weekly that may expire before resolution. (4) Timing rule (hard): NEVER hold the hedge through Union Budget or RBI policy (reliable IV-crush). Enter convexity only when calm is breaking AND a genuine fat-tail catalyst exists — operationally, a contested national election with India VIX still cheap (2024 entered ~8-mid-teens). Demand a statistical cheapness signal (India VIX low vs its mean-reverting ~13-17 post-2014 band, VRP currently positive), not a crash narrative. (5) Cost discipline: cap at ~1-2%/yr but recognize the fixed-brokerage reality — concentrate into fewer, larger orders (more lots/order) to dilute the Rs 20/order drag, and model the full stack (Rs 20/order + 0.10% sell-side STT + 0.03553% exchange + GST 18% on brokerage+exch + 0.003% buy-side stamp). (6) QC/backtest+live path: there is no native route. Use QC for research/signal only with custom NSE option data + self-computed Greeks (QuantLib/py_vollib) + a custom FeeModel encoding the above stack; OR prototype the logic on QC-native US SPX/VIX options as a proxy (accepting smaller India VRP and different costs). Execute live via Kite Connect (Zerodha) outside LEAN, since the QC Zerodha plugin does not route F&O. Treat the strategy as a discrete, calendar-gated tail trade, not an always-on portfolio overlay.

lenses.key_reasoning:
The candidate is empirically robust as a COST-and-TIMING discipline, but it conflates two instruments whose regime behavior differs, and one of its own counterclaims is mischaracterized. Verified directly against the primary texts in /Users/prateekrastogi/app/threat-poc/vol_research/text/.

WHERE IT HOLDS (regimes where the principle is correct):
1. The variance-risk-premium headwind is real and monotonic in calm. Sinclair Table 4.3 (verified, sinclair.txt L3567-3574): implied/realized spread is +27.75% at VIX<20, +19.78% (20-30), +13.73% (30-40), +9.26% (40-50), and only goes NEGATIVE (-11.52%) at VIX>50. Crucially that inverting regime is just 56 of ~5,601 days (~1%). So a tail hedge bought-and-held across the cycle bleeds 99% of the time and only the rare VIX>50 panic repays it. This is the single strongest empirical anchor and it fully supports the strict-cost-cap + buy-into-breaking-calm thesis.
2. The rolling-cost claim is exact, not approximate. Bennett Figure 17 (verified, trading_vol.txt L1365-1366): SX5E rolling ATM put protection cost/yr = 27.7% (1M), 19.6% (2M), 16.0% (3M), 11.3% (6M), 8.0% (1Y). Candidate's '~28% vs 8%' is verbatim. The 1-2% max-acceptable-spend cap is Bennett 1.5 verbatim (L1092).
3. Standalone unprofitability holds out-of-sample and out-of-text. Sinclair Ch.11 p.214 (verified L10474-10475): Broadie/Chernov/Johannes (2009) show even including the 1987 crash, put buying was unprofitable. Independent confirmation: CBOE PPUT (5% OTM monthly put-protection) since 1986 returned 6.64%/yr vs 9.54% for PutWrite, Sharpe 0.33 vs 0.65, MaxDD -38.9% vs -32.7%, 80-month drawdown; academic optimization studies put the put-overlay drag at ~268 bps/yr, attributed explicitly to the variance risk premium. Not curve-fit.
4. Maturity-matching timing is empirically grounded. Bennett Figure 16 (verified L1351-1354): DAX declines since 1960 — Crash -31%/~1mo, Correction -14%/~3mo, Bear -44%/~2.5yr. The 'maturity matches feared decline duration' rule and the '20% fall+recovery: rolling 3M pays, 1Y put expires worthless' example are both in-text (L1330-1334). Far-OTM lottery buyers lose: Sinclair narrative-fallacy section (L10298-10300, Bakshi/Kapadia, Coval/Shumway, Bondarenko, Hodges-Tompkins-Ziemba) — verified.

WHERE IT NEEDS A CAVEAT (the load-bearing flaw):
The principle's first sentence equates 'long-vol/variance' with 'long index puts' and imports Bennett 3.2's '-70% correlation / poor leaky hedge' conclusion onto puts. But Bennett 3.2 is explicitly scoped to NON-CONVEX instruments — variance swaps and rolling VIX/vStoxx futures — and ends with the heading 'HEDGING WITH VARIANCE IS NOT COMPARABLE TO PUTS... the lack of convexity for long volatility makes this an unfair comparison' (verified L3385-3389). The -70% correlation and the 'compare to futures not puts' framing apply to the linear-vol sleeve; a long PUT is convex and DOES own the gap. So the candidate is right that a variance/VIX-futures overlay is a poor hedge, but overstates the case against a convex OTM-put tail hedge, which the conflicting citations (Sinclair Ch.1 'jumps cannot be hedged... replication fails utterly... cap tail risk by trading far-OTM options', verified L980-1011; Bennett 2.3 dealers left short tail variance because they can only hedge with ~ATM options, verified L2289-2291) correctly defend. Net: the cost/timing/sizing discipline holds; the blanket 'puts are a poor leaky hedge' framing is half-right and should be narrowed to non-convex vol.
failure_modes: LONG CALM BLEED (the base case, ~99% of days): At VIX<20 the premium is +27.75% (Table 4.3). A naive rolling 1M ATM put hedge loses ~28%/yr of premium spent; even a 1Y put loses ~8%/yr. India VIX mean-reverts to ~15, so an always-on NIFTY-put tail hedge bleeds through the long droughts between the rare 2008/COVID/2024-election spikes. This is where standalone tail hedging dies by a thousand cuts (PPUT 80-month drawdown).

BUYING AT PEAK FEAR (timing inversion): Buying convexity when VIX is already 40-50 still pays +9.26% premium to the seller, and the inversion only appears at VIX>50 (just ~1% of history). Chasing a crash narrative after the spike overpays and then suffers the IV crush on the snap-back — the exact opposite of 'buy when calm is just breaking.'

INSTRUMENT MISMATCH / linear-vol trap: If the trader expresses 'tail hedge' via a non-convex vol proxy (variance-swap-like exposure, or in a US context VIX futures / VXX) instead of convex OTM puts, Bennett 3.2 applies in full: -70% correlation, -87% rolling-futures decay, no gap ownership. The candidate's framing risks endorsing exactly the wrong instrument because it blurs 'long-vol/variance' with 'long puts.'

FAR-OTM LOTTERY-TICKET BLEED: Buying the deep wings on a crash narrative — Sinclair/Hodges-Tompkins-Ziemba show returns get worse the farther OTM. On NSE the far-OTM NIFTY wings are also the least liquid strikes (thin OI, wide spreads, large slippage per ground.json microstructure topic), so realized cost exceeds modeled cost and the hedge is hard to monetize at the spike.

SCHEDULED-EVENT IV-CRUSH TRAP (NSE-specific): Buying NIFTY straddles/puts INTO Budget or RBI policy is usually a loser — India VIX systematically inflates then crushes (Budget ~-9% avg). Only genuinely binary, poll-defying events (2024 election) reward holding convexity through the event. Misclassifying a crushable event as an inverting one bleeds the hedge.

GRANULARITY / SIZING BLOWUP ON SMALL ACCOUNTS (NSE-specific): SEBI lot sizes (~Rs 15-17 lakh notional/lot, NIFTY lot ~65-75) mean a sub-Rs-15-lakh book cannot fine-tune hedge ratio — forced into chunky over/under-hedging, and STT/flat-brokerage make cheap far-OTM tails cost 2%+ per round trip (ground.json transaction-cost topic), blowing the 1-2% annual cost cap with a single mistimed roll.

NO-INDIA-VIX CONSTRAINT: There is no tradable India VIX future/option/ETP (defunct since 2016), so the hedge MUST be built from NIFTY puts directly. The trader cannot diversify the tail sleeve across instruments, concentrating basis/timing risk into one illiquid wing.
lenses.required_modifications: ["REGIME FILTER (entry): Only initiate/add tail convexity when (a) realized and implied vol are LOW and a vol percentile/cone sits in the bottom decile (India VIX ~13-14 or below), AND (b) you can point to a near-term, genuinely binary, poll-defying catalyst — not a generic crash narrative. Do NOT add when VIX>30 (you are paying +13-19% premium) and never chase after VIX>40-50. This operationalizes 'buy when calm is just breaking, not at peak fear.'", "INSTRUMENT RULE: Express the tail hedge ONLY through convex long OPTIONS (OTM NIFTY puts / put ladders), never through a linear/non-convex vol proxy. The candidate's principle should be re-scoped: 'long VARIANCE/VIX-futures is a poor hedge (Bennett 3.2); long convex PUTS own the gap (Sinclair Ch.1) but are expensive carry — budget them tightly.' Drop the blanket 'puts are a poor leaky hedge' wording.", "STRICT COST CAP + ACCOUNTING: Hard-cap total tail-hedge spend at 1-2% of portfolio/yr (Bennett 1.5). On NSE, build a custom fee model that bakes in STT (0.1%->0.15%), exchange charge, SEBI fee, GST, stamp duty — far-OTM tails run 2%+ round-trip, so price the cap on ALL-IN cost, not premium. Always square off winners in-market (never exercise) to dodge intrinsic-value STT.", "MATURITY-MATCH RULE: Choose tenor by the feared decline's duration (Bennett Fig 16): ~1M for a feared crash, ~3M for a correction, longer-dated for a bear-market thesis. Do not default to cheap 1M rolls (27.7%/yr bleed) unless you specifically fear a sharp short shock; prefer 3-6M to cut roll-frequency friction. Match the put maturity to the catalyst window (e.g. expiry spanning the election result).", "CHEAPEN STRUCTURE, DON'T NAKED-BUY: Use put SPREADS / put-spread collars / calendar collars (Bennett: 'short vol against long put performs well,' L1320) to sell back some of the overpriced premium and stay inside the cost cap — accepting partial protection. Avoid naked far-OTM lottery wings (worst expected return + worst NSE liquidity).", "SIZING TO SURVIVE: Size the sleeve so the maximum annual bleed (cost cap) is a pre-committed, survivable drawdown to the rest of the book; on small NSE accounts where one lot is too chunky to hedge precisely, accept that a clean tail hedge may be infeasible and consider de-risking the underlying (reduce position / hold cash) instead of buying a mistimed put — Bennett's core point that reducing exposure can beat hedging.", "DEMAND MISPRICING, MONITOR EDGE-DECAY: Require a statistical signal (IV cheap vs vol cone / term-structure cheap / forecast RV > IV) before buying, not a narrative. Treat the VIX>50 inversion (Table 4.3) as the only regime where the hedge is structurally cheap, and re-validate the India premium periodically (it is smaller than the US: ~+1.2 vol pts, positive ~75% of days vs ~+3-4 pts/85% US), so the bleed is somewhat lower but still positive — the discipline still binds."]

lenses.key_reasoning:
The candidate is internally contradictory AND structurally hostile to a retail NSE long-vol mandate, on two independent grounds.

(1) SIGN/REGIME CONTRADICTION (fatal, instrument-agnostic). A long calendar in Bennett's convention (long far / short near) is long vega but SHORT GAMMA and SHORT the near leg. The principle says to "favor this when the term structure is inverted/backwardated (near > far), which typically coincides with stress." But stress is exactly when realized volatility explodes and the underlying gaps — the worst possible environment for a short-gamma book. NSE backwardation events are precisely the gap events: Jun-4-2024 election result day NIFTY -6% (~1,379 pts) with VIX +27.7% intraday ~31; COVID Mar-2020. A short-gamma long calendar gets run over by the realized move on entry, while the vega gain is partially offset because in inversion the near (short) leg's IV is ABOVE the far (long) leg, so the short leg is the rich one — the vega edge is actually in being short near, not long far. The structure you want for "own the cheapest expiry" in an inverted curve is closer to a REVERSE calendar (short far / long near = long gamma), which is short vega and therefore NOT a long-vol expression at all. Either way the stated "long far / short near + enter on inversion" recipe is not a coherent long-vol trade; it harvests theta/VRP in calm contango and bleeds in the stress it claims to favor. For a trader whose mandate is LONG volatility (own gamma/vega through tail events), a short-gamma calendar is the wrong sign of exposure into the only events that pay.

(2) NSE/QC EXECUTION makes even a corrected version uneconomic for retail. Calendars need two liquid expiries on the same underlying. Post-SEBI (one weekly per exchange, eff. 20 Nov 2024), NIFTY is the ONLY NSE name with weeklies; everything else is monthly-only, so the cheapest near/far pairs that make calendars interesting (weekly vs monthly) exist on NIFTY alone. On the ~220-230 single stocks and on BANKNIFTY/FINNIFTY/MIDCPNIFTY, you are limited to monthly-vs-monthly (near/next/far, 3 cycles), and the far-month single-stock book is thin (OI/volume concentrate in the near month), so the long far leg fills at wide spreads. Margin: SEBI removed the calendar-spread margin benefit on the near leg's EXPIRY DAY for INDEX derivatives eff. 10 Feb 2025 (you must post full margin on both legs from EOD the day before), and the 2% expiry-day Extreme Loss Margin on short options (eff. 20 Nov 2024) adds ~Rs 39,300/lot on a NIFTY short near leg at ~26,200 — so holding the short-near leg into expiry roughly doubles capital and adds a hard ELM hit exactly when the calendar's theta is supposed to pay. Single-stock options are physically settled (DNE only covers ~3 CTM strikes), so a short-near stock leg risks delivery. India VIX is untradable (futures dead since ~Feb 2016; no options/ETF), so there is no clean term-structure instrument — variance/IV term structure must be self-built from the NIFTY book. QuantConnect has NO native NSE options data and its Zerodha/Samco plugins are equity/commodity-only (no F&O routing), so both backtest and live require a full custom-data + custom-FeeModel build with no native OptionChain/Greeks/fill engine.
failure_modes: Short-gamma blow-up on entry: entering the long calendar precisely on inversion/stress (as the principle directs) puts you short gamma into NSE gap events (Jun-4-2024 NIFTY -6%/VIX +28%, COVID) — the realized move overwhelms the vega gain and the trade loses on the very signal it trades.

Expiry-day margin shock: holding the short near leg into its Tuesday (NIFTY) expiry triggers loss of calendar margin benefit (index, eff 10 Feb 2025) + 2% ELM (~Rs 39,300/NIFTY lot) — capital roughly doubles and ELM bites exactly when theta is supposed to accrue, gutting carry.

Single-stock physical delivery: a short near-month stock-option leg held to expiry can be assigned into compulsory delivery; DNE only covers ~3 CTM strikes, so an ITM short leg forces unwanted stock delivery/funding.

Far-leg illiquidity outside NIFTY: on single stocks and BANKNIFTY/FINNIFTY/MIDCPNIFTY the long far-month leg is thin (OI concentrates near month), so the long-vega leg fills wide and the spread is hard to roll/exit at the bid you modeled.

Wrong-sign vega in inversion: in true backwardation the near (short) leg's IV exceeds the far (long) leg's, so a long-far/short-near calendar is partly short the rich vol and long the cheap vol — vega P&L can be adverse, not the assumed long-vol tailwind.

No term-structure instrument / no premium harvest base: India VIX untradable and VRP is smaller than the US (~+1.2 vol pts mean, positive ~75% but inverts ~25% incl. stress) — the calm-regime theta the corrected calendar would rely on is thin and self-built term-structure z-scores are noisy.

Backtest/live infeasibility on QC: no native NSE options data and equity-only broker plugins mean no turnkey backtest or live F&O routing; a hand-rolled custom-data + custom-fee/fill stack is required, with high risk of mis-modeled fills on the illiquid far leg.
lenses.required_modifications:
If the goal is genuinely LONG volatility, do NOT run a long calendar — its short-gamma sign is wrong for the tail events you want to own. Concrete NSE-accessible recasts:

(1) ABANDON the calendar for long-vol. Replace with a long-gamma/long-vega expression on NIFTY only: buy near-dated (weekly Tuesday) ATM straddles/strangles ahead of identified positive-long-vol events (general elections, where India VIX historically inflates and PAID long vol Jun-4-2024), and AVOID buying through events that crush (Union Budget day -9% avg over 15 yrs; single-stock earnings where implied move > realized). Size in lots (NIFTY ~65 from Jan-2026 series, ~Rs 16-17 lakh notional) and budget the flat-brokerage tax on cheap tails (a Rs 2 far-OTM costs ~30%+ round-trip in fees — buy fewer, cheaper-relative-to-premium contracts and prefer ATM).

(2) If you insist on a term-structure RV trade, restrict it to NIFTY (the only name with both weekly and monthly expiries) and to weekly-vs-monthly pairs, and NEVER carry the short near leg into its Tuesday expiry — close/roll the short leg at least one full day before expiry to dodge the removed calendar margin benefit (index, 10 Feb 2025) and the 2% ELM (~Rs 39,300/lot). Avoid single-stock calendars entirely (thin far month + physical delivery + only monthly-vs-monthly pairs).

(3) Direction of the term-structure tilt: to be net long vol you want LONG the near (cheap, high-gamma) leg in contango and SHORT the far — but recognize that is short vega; there is no clean long-vega-AND-long-gamma single calendar. Accept the trade-off explicitly per regime rather than the candidate's fixed long-far/short-near rule.

(4) Tooling: build NSE term-structure z-scores (near vs far ATM IV via Black-76, normalized by sqrt-time) yourself from the NIFTY option book — there is no India VIX product and no var swap. For backtesting/execution, either (a) ingest custom NSE options data into LEAN with a custom FeeModel (Rs 20/order, STT 0.10% sell-side premium to 31 Mar 2026 then 0.15% from 1 Apr 2026, exchange 0.03553%/side, 18% GST, stamp 0.003% buy) and roll your own Greeks (QuantLib/py_vollib) and fills — accepting you lose LEAN's native option engine — or (b) pivot the research to QC-native US SPX/VIX options where the calendar/term-structure stack works out of the box, then port only the signal. Do NOT expect Zerodha/Samco QC plugins to route F&O — they are equity/commodity-only; live F&O execution must go through Kite Connect outside LEAN.

lenses.key_reasoning:
The candidate's economic premise is real but its NSE/QC expression is not retail-accessible, and the PRELIM "largely impractical" read is correct — actually it understates the problem. Dispersion (long single-stock vol, short index vol) is structurally short correlation and harvests the index correlation/variance premium. India DOES have a positive index VRP (~+1.2 vol pts, positive ~75% of days) and index options embed a correlation premium that single names lack, so the *sign* of the edge exists. But three independent NSE realities each individually kill the retail expression, and they compound:

(1) LIQUIDITY / BASKET CONSTRUCTION. A clean dispersion basket needs ~15-30 liquid single-name option lines you can continuously delta-hedge. NSE has ~220-230 F&O names but only a thin top tier (Reliance, HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, a handful more) has tradable option liquidity; the vast majority trade as single-stock FUTURES, not options. Single-stock options are a small slice of premium turnover (index options — BANKNIFTY ~38%, NIFTY ~28% in 1HFY25 — dominate; the whole market is overwhelmingly NIFTY/BANKNIFTY index premium). You cannot replicate NIFTY-50's correlation structure with ~5-8 mega-caps; the basket tracking error swamps the correlation edge. India structurally favors single-stock futures over options precisely because the option books are thin.

(2) COST STACK vs EDGE. The edge being harvested is small — India's index VRP is only ~+1.2 vol pts (vs the US's ~4.1), and the *correlation* slice of that is a fraction of it. Against this you pay: ~1%+ round-trip on a liquid ATM single-stock option but far worse on the OTM wings, with the flat Rs 20/order brokerage making cheap/illiquid legs cost 13-30%+ of premium; plus wide single-stock option spreads (blue-chip tight, everything else wide); plus continuous delta-hedging across many names, each hedge incurring STT (0.1% sell-side since Oct-2024) + exchange + GST + the bid-ask on the hedge instrument. The candidate itself flags ~10% round-trip on illiquid names. The correlation edge does NOT clear a multi-name, continuously-hedged cost stack for retail.

(3) PHYSICAL SETTLEMENT — the disqualifier the PRELIM under-weights. ALL NSE single-stock options are PHYSICALLY settled (compulsory delivery since Oct-2019). A dispersion book is many simultaneous short-dated single-stock straddles held for vega; carrying any ITM leg into expiry triggers delivery of full notional shares, margin spikes in the physical-settlement window, and short-delivery auction risk (5%/day penalties). DNE only covers ~3 CTM strikes. Lot notionals are large. For a retail multi-name book this is operationally unmanageable and the tail risk is asymmetric.

(4) HEDGE LEG / VEGA-WEIGHTING. The short index leg (NIFTY) is fine and cheap, but vega-weighting index-vs-basket requires the basket to actually track NIFTY's constituents — impossible with the tradable option subset. BANKNIFTY weeklies are gone (monthly only since Nov-2024) and NIFTY weekly moved to Tuesday (Sep-2025), so even the index leg's roll cadence is constrained.

(5) QUANTCONNECT. QC has NO native NSE options/index/F&O data for backtest and NO F&O execution (Zerodha/Samco plugins are equity/MCX-cash only). You'd have to source single-stock options chains for 15-30 names, compute Greeks yourself, build a custom FeeModel, and hand-roll the whole dispersion+delta-hedge engine off-platform — the part LEAN does best (OptionChain/Greeks/fill) is exactly the part that doesn't work for custom NSE data. There is no realistic retail backtest path on QC.

Net: the principle is sound in theory and the sign of the India correlation premium is real, but the retail-accessible NSE expression does not exist — too few liquid option names to form a basket, costs exceed the edge, physical settlement makes a multi-name short-dated vol book hazardous, and QC cannot source or execute it. Refuted as a retail NSE/QC long-vol expression. (Note also: dispersion is SHORT correlation and explicitly NOT a tail hedge, so it does not even serve the user's long-vol/crash-protection objective.)
failure_modes: Basket tracking error: a 5-8 mega-cap option basket cannot replicate NIFTY-50's correlation structure; residual idiosyncratic/sector exposure dominates the intended short-correlation P&L. This is not noise around the edge — it IS larger than the edge.

Physical-settlement blow-up: any ITM single-stock option leg carried into expiry forces full-notional share delivery; with many names short-dated for vega, the probability that at least one leg is ITM at each expiry is high, producing recurring delivery/margin/auction events. Asymmetric, operationally fatal for retail.

Cost bleed exceeding edge: continuous delta-hedging across many thin single-stock option lines (each with wide spreads + STT + flat Rs 20 brokerage + GST) burns far more than the small correlation slice of India's ~1.2 vol-pt VRP.

Wrong-objective: dispersion is SHORT correlation and loses badly when correlations gap to ~1 in a crash — it is the OPPOSITE of the user's stated long-volatility / tail-protection objective. Even if executable, it does not serve the goal.

Correlation regime risk specific to India: India's market is heavily concentrated/correlated (banks + Reliance dominate index weight ~Reliance 10%, HDFC 6%), so realized single-stock correlation is high and the 'dispersion' (idiosyncratic move) you're long is structurally scarce — the short-correlation bet has thin upside and fat downside.

QC has zero data/execution path: cannot backtest (no NSE options data) and cannot live-trade F&O (equity-only plugins); the bespoke off-platform build needed has no validation harness and high model-risk on self-computed Greeks/fills.

Liquidity vanishes in the wings: the OTM strikes needed for clean vega/correlation exposure on single names are the thinnest and most expensive (13-30%+ round-trip), so the legs that matter most are the least tradable.
lenses.required_modifications: Do not attempt retail dispersion on NSE — refuted as specified. If the user wants the correlation-premium edge, recast it onto QC-NATIVE US options where everything works out-of-the-box: long a basket of liquid US single-stock options (or sector ETFs) vs short SPX/SPY index vol, using AlgoSeek US Equity + Index Options data with LEAN's native OptionChain/Greeks/IV/fill models and code-driven delta hedging. That is the only path that is both backtestable AND has a clean liquidity/settlement (cash-settled SPX) profile. If the user insists on staying in India: (1) Abandon the multi-name basket — it is not retail-buildable. The only defensible India 'correlation' proxy is a TWO-instrument trade: short NIFTY index straddle vs long straddles on the 1-2 most liquid mega-caps (Reliance/HDFC Bank) — but accept this is a crude, high-tracking-error proxy, not true dispersion, and is still short correlation (not a tail hedge). (2) Use only the top ~5 liquid single-stock option names, ATM only (avoid thin OTM wings), and CLOSE every single-stock leg before expiry-week to avoid physical settlement entirely — never carry stock options to expiry. (3) Short leg: NIFTY monthly (not weekly) options to reduce roll/gamma churn; vega-weight against the small basket. (4) Pre-budget IV crush and earnings IV-ramp will distort single-name legs — schedule around scheduled binary events. (5) Backtest is off-QC: source single-stock + NIFTY option chains from an NSE F&O vendor (e.g. TrueData), compute Greeks with QuantLib/py_vollib, model the full cost stack (Rs 20/order + 0.10% sell STT + exchange/GST/stamp + realistic single-stock spreads) in a custom FeeModel/SlippageModel, and run the delta-hedge logic by hand. Honest expectation: even fully built, the costs and physical-settlement risk likely consume the small correlation edge for retail — treat the US recast as the primary recommendation.

lenses.key_reasoning:
This is the rare long-vol insight that gets STRONGER, not weaker, under NSE reality — it is essentially a checklist of the exact frictions that sink naive NSE long-vol backtests. Each sub-claim survives scrutiny:

(1) STT-on-exercise trap is real and the single largest hidden risk. Current rule: 0.10% of premium on sell-side square-off vs 0.125% of INTRINSIC VALUE on ITM exercise/expiry (index options are cash-settled = deemed exercised). The legacy Zerodha example (old contract-value basis) shows ~Rs 562 vs ~Rs 3.75 — a >100x penalty; even on the current intrinsic-value basis a deep-ITM NIFTY option left to expire pays 0.125% of a large intrinsic number vs 0.10% of a small premium. For a long-vol book that BY DESIGN ends up holding ITM gamma winners through expiry, "always square off before expiry" is not optional polish — it is the difference between capturing the convex payoff and donating it to STT. From 1 Apr 2026 exercise STT rises to 0.15%, worsening the trap. Verdict: square-off discipline is mandatory and the candidate is correct.

(2) Flat-brokerage-dominates-cheap-tails is arithmetically correct and has a sharp strategic consequence. Round trip = ~Rs 40 brokerage + ~Rs 7 GST + tiny % levies. On a Rs 2/unit far-OTM tail (Rs 150/lot) that is ~30%+ round-trip cost; on a Rs 100/unit ATM weekly (Rs 7,500/lot) it is ~0.8%. So "prefer liquid ATM index strikes over cheap illiquid wings" is enforced by the cost structure, not just liquidity — the cheapest-looking long-vol tails are the most cost-toxic. This INVERTS the textbook US tail-hedge instinct.

(3) Custom LEAN FeeModel is genuinely mandatory. QC has NO native NSE options data and the Zerodha/Samco plugins are equity/commodity-only (no F&O routing), so there is no stock fee model that captures the NSE options statutory stack at all — you are building the data layer AND the FeeModel from scratch regardless. A FeeModel that omits STT (especially the asymmetric sell-side/exercise STT) will systematically flatter long-vol P&L because long-vol turns over premium at every roll and square-off.

(4) Roll-frequency minimization matters more in India because the harvest cushion is thin. India's VRP averages only ~+1.2 vol pts (positive ~75% of days) vs the US ~+4.1 pts — so a long-vol (premium-PAYING) book is fighting a real negative carry, and every avoidable round-trip (~0.8% ATM, far worse on tails) compounds against it. Wide long-gamma re-hedge bands and minimal rolls are the correct response.

The only adjustments are scoping, not refutation: the candidate must be re-expressed on NIFTY-only weeklies (BANKNIFTY/FINNIFTY/MIDCPNIFTY weeklies were killed Nov-2024; NIFTY weekly is now TUESDAY since 1 Sep 2025), and single-stock long-vol carries an extra physical-settlement/DNE complication that the candidate's "square off" rule already handles but should name explicitly. India VIX being untradable is irrelevant to this cost-management insight (it concerns execution of NIFTY options, not VIX products).</parameter>

failure_modes: Backtest flattery via incomplete FeeModel: omitting STT (or modelling only the symmetric premium-based STT and not the punitive 0.125%-of-intrinsic exercise leg) makes a long-vol backtest show false positive carry. A long-vol book turns over premium at every roll/square-off, so even a small per-trip understatement compounds into a materially overstated Sharpe.

STT exercise trap firing silently in code: if the LEAN custom layer lets a cash-settled ITM NIFTY option reach expiry (deemed exercised), the engine must apply 0.125%-of-intrinsic STT — but since this is custom code, a developer who only books square-off costs will both (a) mis-model history and (b) in live trading actually eat the trap. Must hard-code a 'square off all ITM by T-minus-1 / expiry-morning' rule and mirror it in the FeeModel.

Far-OTM tail toxicity: buying cheap NIFTY wings (Rs 1-3/unit) for convexity looks attractive but costs 13-30%+ round trip from flat brokerage alone, so the convex tail can be net-negative after costs even when the gross thesis is right. Mitigate by trading fewer, larger, nearer-the-money strikes (more lots per order amortises the Rs 20).

Single-stock physical settlement: applying this to optionable NSE single stocks (the candidate mentions 'liquid ATM index strikes over cheap illiquid wings' but a user could stray to single names) risks compulsory physical delivery of the underlying on any ITM stock option left to expire — far worse than an index cash-settle trap. The ~3-strike DNE facility only partially helps. Long-vol should stay on cash-settled NIFTY index options; single-stock skews are also shallower (smaller VRP harvest).

Liquidity/spread on non-NIFTY indices: BANKNIFTY/FINNIFTY/MIDCPNIFTY no longer have weeklies, so any weekly-gamma long-vol expression must be NIFTY-only; routing to discontinued-weekly instruments or stale strikes produces unfillable limit orders and wide realized spreads that the candidate's 'earn the spread with limit orders' assumption cannot overcome.

Live execution gap: even a perfect QC backtest cannot be deployed natively — Zerodha/Samco LEAN plugins do not route F&O, so live orders must go through an external broker API (Kite Connect) outside LEAN, introducing a backtest-vs-live divergence in fills/slippage that the SlippageModel must be conservatively tuned to anticipate.

EXACT NSE EXPRESSION (retail-accessible): Trade NIFTY 50 index options ONLY (cash-settled, so no physical-delivery risk; the single liquid weekly-bearing index after SEBI rationalisation). Long-vol leg = long ATM/near-ATM NIFTY straddle or strangle for gamma/vega; choose strikes within ~1-2 strikes of spot (NOT cheap far-OTM wings — those cost 13-30%+ round trip from flat brokerage). Expiry: current weekly is TUESDAY (since 1 Sep 2025) for short-dated gamma; use the monthly (last Tuesday) for vega/lower-roll-frequency carry. Lot = 65 (Jan-2026 rebaseline), ~Rs 16-17 lakh notional — size position count so the Rs 20/order is amortised across multiple lots. Delta-hedge leg = NIFTY futures (or offsetting NIFTY options), re-hedged on WIDE long-gamma bands (e.g. only when delta drifts beyond a threshold) to minimise round trips. HARD RULES: (1) Square off every ITM/winning option in the market before expiry — never let a cash-settled ITM index option reach deemed-exercise (0.125%-of-intrinsic vs 0.10%-of-premium; rising to 0.15% from 1 Apr 2026). (2) Use LIMIT orders at/inside mid to earn rather than pay the spread. (3) Minimise rolls given the thin ~+1.2-vol-pt VRP cushion = real negative carry.

BACKTEST PATH (QC has no NSE options data): ingest CUSTOM NSE NIFTY options-chain data as a custom data type; compute Greeks/IV yourself (QuantLib / py_vollib) since LEAN's native OptionChain/Greeks engine won't bind to custom contracts; run delta-hedging logic in code. Attach a CUSTOM FeeModel (subclass FeeModel.GetOrderFee) that models: Rs 20/order brokerage, 18% GST on (brokerage + exchange + SEBI), 0.03553% exchange/side, 0.0001% SEBI/side, 0.003% stamp (buy side) — and CRITICALLY an asymmetric STT module: 0.10% of premium on every sell-side square-off AND a separate 0.125%-of-intrinsic charge on any contract allowed to reach expiry ITM, so the backtest both costs the trap and creates the incentive to code the square-off rule. Add a conservative custom SlippageModel/FillModel calibrated to NIFTY weekly spreads. Validate net carry under realistic roll frequency.

LIVE PATH: QC/LEAN for signal + research only; route live F&O orders via external broker API (Kite Connect) outside LEAN, since neither the Zerodha nor Samco LEAN plugin routes F&O. Mirror the square-off-before-expiry logic in the live order manager.

DO NOT: use cheap far-OTM tails as the primary long-vol vehicle; use single-stock options (physical settlement + shallower VRP); rely on India VIX products (untradable); or trust any stock LEAN fee model — there isn't one for NSE options.

lenses.key_reasoning:
The structure-selection PRINCIPLE is mechanically sound and survives NSE reality — long ATM straddles / OTM strangles are the only retail-accessible direction-neutral long-vol vehicle in India (India VIX is untradable: no listed options/ETF, futures dead since ~Feb 2016), and the PRELIM fit is largely correct. But it holds ONLY after three corrections and one hard reframing.

(1) The deeper economic problem the candidate underweights: India runs a POSITIVE volatility risk premium (~+1.2 vol pts, positive ~75% of days), so the long-vol BUYER is paying the premium and is structurally on the losing side of the average trade. Long-vol straddles/strangles are therefore NOT a passive carry harvest — they are a NEGATIVE-carry bet that must be timed to specific realized-vol/event catalysts where IV under-prices the coming move. The "cleanest structure" framing is fine; the framing as a standing position is not.

(2) Event selection is the whole game and the candidate's own VERIFIED fact base contradicts naive event-buying. Scheduled binary events systematically CRUSH IV: Union Budget day (India VIX fell on all 15 budget days 2011-2025, avg >~9%; Feb-2025 -12.21%), RBI policy (milder bleed), and single-stock earnings (IT names crush 35-50% in 24h, implied move > realized) all PUNISH long straddles held through the event. The genuinely long-vol-paying catalysts are FAT-TAIL/unscheduled or genuinely two-sided events — general elections (Jun-4-2024 result: VIX +27.7% to 26.74, NIFTY -6%, textbook gamma payoff), and acute risk-off shocks where term structure inverts. So the applicable expression is: buy gamma into genuinely two-sided/fat-tail events, NOT into scheduled IV-crush events.

(3) The "delta-neutral strike sits slightly above spot" claim is directionally correct but for the wrong reason and partly self-cancelling on NSE. Under lognormal Black-Scholes the spot-delta-neutral straddle strike is K=F·exp(½σ²τ), above the FORWARD (and forward>spot for positive rates) — so yes, above spot. BUT NIFTY's steep reverse put-skew (25d risk-reversal +3 to +6 vol pts) pulls the practical delta-neutral point and makes a spot-ATM straddle slightly NET-SHORT delta via skew, partially offsetting the lognormal drift effect. For a retail trader the refinement is third-order vs. transaction costs; treat ATM-spot as "close enough" and re-center with a small futures/cash delta hedge if running it more than a day.

(4) Structure choice on NSE specifically: on the INDEX (NIFTY) the straddle wins — paise-wide spreads, deepest liquidity, cash-settled (no delivery risk), weekly Tuesday expiry gives a usable short-dated gamma vehicle. The strangle's flatter-vega/wider-gamma advantage is real but its cheap OTM tails get destroyed by the FLAT Rs 20/order brokerage (a Rs 2 far-OTM leg costs ~30%+ round-trip vs ~0.8% for an ATM Rs 100 leg) — so prefer the ATM straddle or only a MILDLY OTM strangle (Rs 30-60 legs, ~13% cost) and batch multiple lots per order. On SINGLE STOCKS the strangle's vega advantage is NOT worth it: monthly-only expiries, physical settlement (delivery/DNE-band risk), shallower skew, lower liquidity and embedded borrow in high-short-interest names make single-name long-vol a worse harvest than the index, which itself carries the larger correlation premium.

(5) Backtest/live path: QC has NO NSE options data and the Zerodha/Samco plugins are EQUITY/COMMODITY segment only (no F&O routing), confirmed. So the candidate's "QC cannot trade these natively; execute via broker API" is correct and is the binding constraint, not a footnote.</parameter>

failure_modes: IV-crush on scheduled events: holding a long straddle/strangle through Union Budget (VIX fell 15/15 years, avg >~9%), RBI policy, or single-stock earnings loses on vega even when direction is right — the most common retail long-vol blow-up in India.

Negative carry / theta bleed on the Tuesday weekly: a NIFTY weekly straddle now has fewer calendar days of gamma per cycle; buying-and-holding pays theta into a positive-VRP market and bleeds unless a catalyst hits inside the window.

Flat Rs 20/order brokerage destroys cheap OTM strangle tails: a Rs 2 far-OTM leg is ~30%+ round-trip cost vs ~0.8% for an ATM Rs 100 leg — the structure's 'cheaper strangle' appeal is largely a cost illusion at the wings; mitigated only by stacking lots per order.

Single-stock physical settlement: a long stock-option leg left ITM at monthly expiry triggers compulsory delivery (full-notional obligation); DNE facility only covers a ~3-strike CTM band and does NOT cover index options — a delivery/margin trap for retail running single-name straddles.

Single-name borrow/liquidity drag: high-short-interest EM single stocks embed borrow cost in put-call parity and have wider spreads + monthly-only expiries, making single-stock long-vol a structurally worse harvest than the index.

Lot-size notional shock: ~Rs 16-17 lakh notional per NIFTY lot means even buyer-only premium outlay (e.g. weekly ATM straddle ~Rs 2-4 lakh/lot in elevated vol) plus position sizing can be large for retail; under-capitalized sizing forces premature exits.

QC execution gap: signals generated in LEAN cannot be auto-routed to NSE F&O (equity/commodity-only plugins), so live trading requires a separate broker-API (Kite Connect) execution layer — introduces slippage/latency/operational risk not modeled in the backtest.
Skew mis-hedging: assuming a spot-ATM straddle is delta-flat ignores NIFTY's steep reverse put skew, leaving a residual short-delta tilt that bleeds in a grind-up market.
lenses.required_modifications: EXACT NSE EXPRESSION: (a) Vehicle = NIFTY 50 INDEX options only (cash-settled, paise-wide, deepest liquidity); avoid single-stock straddles (monthly-only, physical settlement, DNE only covers ~3 CTM strikes and not relevant to index). (b) Expiry = NIFTY weekly TUESDAY-expiry contract for event/gamma bets; choose the expiry that BRACKETS the catalyst by 1-2 sessions, not the 0-1 DTE that has no gamma window left. (c) Strikes/structure = long ATM straddle at the nearest strike to spot (treat spot-ATM as the practical delta-neutral point; the lognormal K=F*exp(1/2 sigma^2 tau) drift above forward is offset by reverse put-skew, so do not over-engineer). If using a strangle, keep legs only MILDLY OTM (premium ~Rs 30-60, ~5-13% round-trip cost), never deep Rs 2-5 tails that the flat Rs 20/order brokerage destroys; batch multiple lots per order to amortize brokerage. (d) Hedge leg = optional small delta re-center with NIFTY futures (or by skewing strike) if held >1 day, to neutralize the skew-induced short-delta tilt. ENTRY DISCIPLINE (the core fix): only initiate when IV is CHEAP relative to expected realized move AND the catalyst is genuinely two-sided/fat-tail (general elections, unscheduled macro shocks, regime-inversion of term structure) — explicitly AVOID buying gamma into Budget day, RBI policy, and single-stock earnings, which systematically crush IV; on those, the verified evidence favors the short-premium side. Size to premium-at-risk per ~Rs 16-17 lakh-notional lot, not to notional. BACKTEST PATH: QC has NO NSE options data and Greeks/OptionChain/fill engine is wired to native Option SecurityType, so either (1) import custom NSE NIFTY options-chain data as a custom type, compute Greeks/IV yourself (QuantLib/py_vollib) and run delta-hedging in code, attaching a custom FeeModel (Rs 20/order + 0.10% sell-side STT + 0.03553% exchange + 18% GST on brokerage+exchange+SEBI + 0.003% buy-side stamp) and custom Fill/Slippage models — accepting you lose the turnkey options stack; or (2) prototype the long-vol LOGIC on QC-native US SPX/VIX options (full chains/Greeks out-of-the-box) and re-fit frictions to NSE. LIVE PATH: QC/LEAN for signal+research only; route orders via Zerodha Kite Connect API OUTSIDE LEAN (the Zerodha/Samco LEAN plugins are equity/commodity-segment only and cannot place F&O orders).

lenses.key_reasoning:
I tried to refute this on three fronts — factual accuracy of the NSE claim, correctness of the US vol-product mechanism, and whether the edge is real vs. data-mined/overstated — and it survives all three, but it needs relabeling because it is a structural constraint dressed up as a long-vol "insight," not an alpha edge.

(A) NSE leg is factually correct and is the load-bearing, fully-defensible core. India VIX is a published index computed off NIFTY option prices (CBOE-licensed methodology), not a tradable instrument. NSE did launch India VIX futures (weekly contracts, Feb 2014), but they never developed liquidity and are effectively defunct; there are no India VIX options and no VIX ETPs in India. Independent sources confirm the practitioner consensus: "You cannot directly trade India VIX... you can use Nifty options strategies to express a view on volatility." So the operative conclusion — all NSE long-vol must be built from NIFTY/stock options themselves — is not an empirical edge that can be out-of-sample-fragile; it is a description of the available instrument set. This part cannot be "data-mined" and is the strongest claim in the candidate.

(B) US leg (VXX contango bleed) is textbook-correct and quantitatively well-calibrated, not overstated. The mechanism is right: a constant-maturity vol index cannot be statically replicated (sqrt concavity + daily rolling), cash VIX is non-deliverable so VIX futures carry no cash-and-carry arbitrage and are model/expectations-priced, and a fund rolling M1->M2 daily mechanically buys higher/sells lower along an upward curve. The empirics back the magnitude: VIX futures are in contango ~80-85% of the time (one source: 85.2% Jan-2013 to Nov-2024; M1<M2 ~82% since inception), and unleveraged long products like VXX/VIXY bleed roughly 40-60%/yr in calm regimes (leveraged UVXY 60-80%). The candidate's own ~(1/30)(F2-F1)/day daily-roll formula is the correct first-order drag for a 30-day constant-maturity product, and Bennett's rolling-1M-vStoxx -87% vs equity -4% is a real, directionally-consistent illustration of the same effect in Europe. If anything the candidate is conservative, not overstated.

(C) The counterclaims bound rather than defeat. Long VIX futures DO make money in backwardation (IVTS>1 / curve-inversion timing), calendar put spreads and long-VXZ/short-VXX (Donninger) monetize or finance the contango with bounded risk, and options on vol futures have positive skew so long VIX calls are a genuine convex tail hedge where they exist. None of these contradict the principle — they all presuppose a liquid futures/options vol-product ecosystem, which is exactly what India lacks. They sharpen the US-side claim ("tactical, not buy-and-hold") and are simply inapplicable to NSE.

The only real defect is framing/altitude: this is sold as a long-volatility *insight* but its actual content is a *negative result / instrument-availability constraint* ("there is no VIX product to be long in India; the US analog decays if held"). It tells you what NOT to do and where the carry leaks, not how to capture a long-vol edge. That is useful and correct but should not be scored as an alpha thesis.
failure_modes: Mislabeling: presented as a long-vol 'insight'/edge when it is actually a negative result + instrument-availability constraint. It tells you what is unavailable/decaying, not how to harvest a long-vol edge. Scoring it as alpha overstates it.

Survivorship/regime overstatement on the US side if misread: VXX is NOT a one-way short; the contango bleed reverses sharply in stress (Feb 2018, Mar 2020) when the curve inverts. Stating the decay without flagging that the SHORT-VXX trade carries unbounded crash risk would be the real overstatement. The candidate avoids this via its counterclaims, but a reader could over-extrapolate 'VXX always bleeds' into 'short VXX is free money.'

Overpricing/risk-premium claim is regime- and period-dependent: the 'historically overpriced' variance/vol-risk-premium is not a stable constant; it compressed post-2017 and the short-vol crowding that funded it blew up. Treating it as a durable structural rent would be data-mined.

'Futures effectively defunct since Feb 2016' is the weakest specific number — the broad fact (illiquid/discontinued, no options, no ETPs) is well-supported, but I could not independently confirm the exact 'last meaningful trade Feb 2016' date from public sources; treat that precise date as plausible-but-uncorroborated.

Cross-market transfer risk: the US contango/roll-decay empirics do NOT transfer to NSE as a tradable phenomenon precisely because the products don't exist there; using US VXX statistics to imply anything about an NSE position would be a category error (the candidate correctly does not do this, but it's a latent trap).
lenses.required_modifications: ["Relabel from 'long-vol insight' to 'long-vol instrument constraint / negative result.' The actionable core is: on NSE, long-vol can ONLY be expressed through NIFTY/stock options (long straddles/strangles, calendars, ratio backspreads, VIX-proxy via option structures) — there is no index product to be long. Keep this as the primary, high-confidence claim.", "On the US side, add the explicit symmetry caveat the principle implies but does not state: the contango bleed is the flip side of a short-vol crash risk. 'VXX is not buy-and-hold long' must be paired with 'short VXX/short-vol is not buy-and-hold either — it carries unbounded tail risk (Feb 2018, Mar 2020).' Otherwise the asymmetry reads as a one-way edge.", "Qualify 'futures overpriced' as regime/period-dependent vol-risk-premium, not a structural constant; note compression post-2017 and that the premium is itself the compensation for crash risk, so it is not free.", "Soften the precise 'last meaningful trade Feb 2016' to 'illiquid/discontinued, no options, no ETPs' unless the exact date can be sourced from NSE archives; the broad structural claim is what carries the weight.", "Make the counterclaims part of the surviving statement, not exceptions: where a liquid vol-product ecosystem exists (US), long-vol is achievable tactically via (i) long futures only in backwardation/IVTS>1, (ii) calendar put spreads / long-VXZ-short-VXX to finance carry, (iii) long VIX calls as a convex tail hedge exploiting positive vol-of-vol skew. Explicitly note these are exactly the tools India lacks, which is the whole point of the principle."]

lenses.key_reasoning:
The core principle is mechanically sound and survives NSE reality, but the candidate's specific "edge" claims are partly wrong and must be inverted, so it holds only after material modification.

WHAT HOLDS: The Whalley-Wilmott / Zakamouline no-trade band is a genuine closed-form result and is trivially codeable in LEAN. Confirmed half-band half-width H = ((3·c·Γ²·S)/(2·a))^(1/3) around the BS delta, where c = proportional round-trip cost rate, Γ = position gamma, S = spot, a = risk-aversion (Zakamouline adds a vol-adjusted variant). It is a free, accurate approximation in the small-cost regime (c ≲ ~1%). The qualitative asymmetry the candidate states is also correct: for a LONG-gamma book you can run a WIDER band than short-gamma because convexity self-insures — every adverse move you DIDN'T hedge is partly recovered by the long gamma, whereas a short-gamma book bleeds quadratically if you sit. So "don't hedge continuously, rehedge to the band edge, widen for long gamma" is correct and worth implementing. Given that India does run a positive but SMALL index VRP (~+1.2 vol pts, positive ~75% of days — far thinner than the US ~4 pt cushion) and inverts ~25% of the time, a long-gamma (long-straddle/long-option) book is the natural vehicle here, and the dominant determinant of whether long gamma is net profitable is precisely how cheaply you can monetise the gamma via re-hedging. So band sizing is not cosmetic — it is the make-or-break P&L lever. Verdict therefore holds: the insight is applicable and can be net-additive.

WHAT MUST BE CORRECTED (why caveats, not clean hold):

1) The candidate's headline edge — "sit on bid/offer on wide-spread NSE single names, re-hedge with limit orders to EARN rather than PAY the spread" — is largely misapplied for the practical retail hedge. The natural, retail-accessible delta-hedge instrument for an index long-gamma book is the NIFTY near-month FUTURE, which is one of the most liquid contracts in the world and trades at a 1-tick (essentially zero) spread. There is no spread to "earn" on the index future — the spread-capture thesis evaporates exactly where execution is reliable. The wide spreads the candidate wants to harvest exist on SINGLE-STOCK futures/options, but those are physically settled (delivery risk), carry the smaller/noisier single-name VRP, and have far worse liquidity — so "sit on the offer and get filled" is unreliable there. Net: the spread-earning edge is real only on illiquid single names where fill is unreliable, and absent on the liquid index where fill is reliable. This is the central inversion.

2) Cost-stack timing bites the hedge leg. The hedge is done in FUTURES, and futures sell-side STT was 0.02% through 31 Mar 2026 but ROSE to 0.05% effective 1 Apr 2026 (Budget 2026-27) — a 2.5x jump landing inside the strategy's own 2025-26 window. Plus flat Rs 20/order brokerage + 18% GST + exchange/SEBI/stamp. Each re-hedge round-trip on a NIFTY future therefore costs a fixed Rs ~40 brokerage + GST + ~0.05% of sell notional (≈ Rs 800 on a ~Rs 16L lot at the new rate). This RAISES the effective c in the WW formula, which (correctly) WIDENS the optimal band — fewer, larger re-hedges — but also directly eats long-gamma P&L. The band must be re-solved with c reflecting the post-Apr-2026 futures STT, not the equity-option cost stack quoted for buyers.

3) Lot granularity forces the band wide anyway, and this DESTROYS most of the modeled benefit at retail scale. The minimum hedge increment is ONE NIFTY future lot = 65 units ≈ Rs 16-17 lakh notional ≈ ~65 deltas. The WW band edge is continuous; you can only rehedge in integer-lot quanta. For a small retail book (e.g. 1-3 long straddle lots), the position's total delta swing across a normal day is often smaller than one futures lot, so the "rehedge to band edge" instruction frequently rounds to "do nothing" or "flip a whole lot" — the band's fine structure is unobservable below lot granularity. The benefit survives meaningfully only for a book large enough that 65-delta increments are small relative to total gamma exposure (roughly tens of lots of options), which is above most retail size. So the candidate's "lot granularity means the band is forced wide anyway" is true but it is a BUG, not a feature: it means the WW optimisation is largely moot at retail size and you're really running a coarse heuristic.

4) Tooling: QuantConnect has NO NSE options/futures DATA and NO native F&O order routing (Zerodha/Samco plugins are equity/MCX-cash only). India VIX is untradable (no futures/options/ETF). So neither backtest nor live can be done natively. You must either build a full custom-data + custom-FeeModel + self-computed-Greeks layer in LEAN (losing the turnkey OptionChain/Greeks engine) and execute live via Kite Connect outside LEAN, or pivot research to QC-native US SPX/SPY where the entire stack works out of the box and the WW band logic is portable verbatim.

NET: the band methodology holds; the long-gamma asymmetry holds; the specific "earn the spread on wide NSE names" edge is refuted for the practical index hedge and only weakly true on illiquid single names; and the retail-size benefit is heavily eroded by lot granularity and the Apr-2026 futures-STT hike. Hence holds-with-caveats, medium confidence.
failure_modes: Spread-capture illusion: assuming you can 'sit on the bid/offer and earn the spread' when hedging an index book — the NIFTY near-month future has ~1-tick spreads, so there is nothing to earn; limit-order hedging there just risks non-fill and unhedged drift. The wide-spread names where capture is possible (single-stock futures) carry physical-delivery risk and unreliable fills, so the limit order may simply never fill and leave you naked-delta into a move.

Lot-granularity dominance at retail size: with a 65-unit NIFTY lot (~65 deltas, ~Rs 16-17L), a small long-straddle book's daily delta swing is often smaller than one hedge lot, so 'rehedge to band edge' rounds to either no-op or a full-lot flip — the WW band's fine structure is unobservable and the optimisation collapses into a coarse heuristic. Forces minimum effective book size into the tens-of-lots range, above typical retail.

Apr-2026 futures-STT hike (0.02%→0.05% sell-side) silently raises c mid-window; a band calibrated on pre-Apr-2026 or on the equity-option buyer cost stack will over-hedge and bleed. The flat Rs 20/order brokerage also makes frequent small re-hedges punitive — the cost model must be lot- and date-aware.

Thin/inverting VRP: India's ~+1.2 vol-pt premium inverts ~25% of the time and narrows going INTO shocks; a long-gamma book pays theta against a thin edge, so even optimal banding may not rescue net-negative carry outside event windows. Long gamma pays only when realized > implied, which is the minority regime.

Physical settlement trap on single names: if the candidate is pushed onto single-stock options to chase wide spreads, ITM legs at expiry trigger compulsory delivery (DNE facility only covers ~3 CTM strikes), creating margin/delivery blowups a delta-hedging retail account is not set up for.

Tooling dead-ends: no NSE options data in QC means no faithful backtest of the band's gamma-dependent re-hedge schedule without building a custom-data/Greeks/fee layer; LEAN's native OptionChain/Greeks won't attach to custom-imported contracts (AddOptionContract KeyErrors). Live, neither Zerodha nor Samco LEAN plugin routes F&O, so execution must go through Kite Connect outside LEAN — backtest and live diverge.

Overnight/0DTE gamma cliff on NIFTY weeklies (now Tuesday expiry, NIFTY-only after BANKNIFTY weeklies were discontinued): a band sized on continuous-time gamma understates the discontinuous delta jump as 0DTE gamma evaporates; sitting in-band overnight near expiry can produce a large unhedged gap.
lenses.required_modifications:
EXACT RETAIL-ACCESSIBLE NSE EXPRESSION:
- Long-vol/long-gamma core: BUY NIFTY 50 index options only (weekly Tuesday expiry, NIFTY is the only index with weeklies post-Nov-2024; monthly last-Tuesday for longer horizon). Use ATM or slightly-OTM straddle/strangle on a HORIZON that targets event-rich windows where realized tends to beat the thin VRP — e.g. carry gamma THROUGH general-election result days (Jun-4-2024 paid a textbook long-gamma payoff) and AVOID Union Budget day / scheduled events where IV reliably crushes (sell, don't buy, those). Stay on the index, not single stocks (cleaner/larger VRP, cash-settled, no delivery risk).
- Hedge leg: delta-hedge with the NIFTY NEAR-MONTH FUTURE (cash-settled, ~1-tick spread, retail-accessible), NOT single-stock futures. Drop the 'earn the spread' tactic on the index — instead minimise round-trips. You may still post marketable-limit (not pure passive) orders to cap slippage, but do not model spread income.
- Band: implement Whalley-Wilmott H = ((3·c·Γ²·S)/(2·a))^(1/3) about BS delta; rehedge ONLY when net delta exits ±H, and only back to the band EDGE (not to zero). For the long-gamma book, use the asymmetric/wider band. Set c to the REALISTIC post-1-Apr-2026 futures round-trip: 0.05% sell-side STT + Rs 20/order brokerage + 18% GST on brokerage+exchange + 0.03553% exchange + stamp — i.e. a lot- and date-conditional c, recomputed per re-hedge.
- Lot quantisation: round the band-edge target hedge to the nearest INTEGER NIFTY future lot (65 units). Only run the strategy at a book size large enough (roughly tens of option lots) that 65-delta increments are small vs total gamma; below that, accept the WW band degenerates to a coarse 'hedge when delta exceeds ~half a lot' heuristic and size expectations accordingly.

BACKTEST + LIVE PATH (given QC has no NSE F&O data/execution):
- Backtest: QC native path is unavailable. Two options. (A) Build a LEAN custom-data layer: import NSE NIFTY options + near-month futures bars as custom types, compute Greeks/IV yourself (QuantLib/py_vollib), run the WW band + lot-rounded hedge logic in code, and attach a custom FeeModel (subclass FeeModel.GetOrderFee) encoding the date-aware STT/brokerage/GST/exchange/stamp stack plus a custom Fill/SlippageModel — you lose the turnkey OptionChain/Greeks engine. (B) PREFERRED for validating the methodology: pivot the backtest to QC-NATIVE US SPX/SPXW options + ES/SPY hedge (AlgoSeek, from 2012), where chains, Greeks, IV, fee/fill models and code-driven delta hedging all work out-of-the-box; the WW band code is portable verbatim — then re-skin costs/lots/VRP to NSE for the India read.
- Live: execute outside LEAN via Zerodha Kite Connect API (the LEAN Zerodha/Samco plugins do NOT route F&O); use QC/LEAN only for signal/Greeks/band computation and fire option + NIFTY-future hedge orders through Kite. India VIX is untradable, so all 'vol' exposure is via NIFTY options; use India VIX only as a cheapness/regime gauge to time entries.

lenses.key_reasoning:
The core principle is fundamentally correct and survives NSE reality: for scheduled binary events (elections, Budget, RBI, single-stock earnings), the payoff is a GAP/jump that dynamic delta hedging mathematically cannot replicate — you must own the jump via long options, and dynamic hedging into the print only bleeds the convexity you paid for. This is the one regime where long-vol/tail buying is structurally defensible in India DESPITE the positive VRP (NIFTY IV > realized ~75% of days, +1.2 vol pts mean), because (a) the VRP inverts during genuine fat-tail shocks and elections specifically PAY long vol through the event (Jun-4-2024: India VIX +27.7% to 26.74, intraday ~31/+51%, NIFTY -6%), and (b) the edge is event SELECTION, not holding vol generically.

However, the candidate's recommended mechanics need heavy NSE-specific correction, which is why this is holds-WITH-CAVEATS rather than clean holds:

1. INSTRUMENT CHOICE IS REGIME-SPLIT. For INDEX events (Budget/RBI/election) the right vehicle is NIFTY — the ONLY index with weekly options post-SEBI-rationalisation (BANKNIFTY/FINNIFTY/MIDCPNIFTY weeklies all dead since Nov-2024; expiry now TUESDAY since Sep-1-2025), cash-settled, deep multi-strike book that genuinely supports static structures (wings, calendars).
