adjudicators.net_actionable: Trade long vol ONLY as a selective, de-biased NIFTY-index expression: before calling any NIFTY option "cheap," subtract an India-sized, REGIME-CONDITIONAL haircut of ~+1 to +1.5 vol points (NOT Sinclair's US ~3.09) from live NIFTY ATM IV vs your trailing 30-day Yang-Zhang realized vol; only initiate when the de-biased spread is at/below that threshold AND either India VIX is in the lower part of its post-2014 band (~13 or below) or a genuine fat-tail catalyst (general election) resolves through the position. Never buy-and-hold; never use the index haircut on single stocks; flip to favoring long vol (no haircut) once India VIX is in panic territory where the premium goes negative.
conditions: Apply ONLY to NIFTY 50 index options (the sole NSE index with weeklies, cash-settled, no delivery risk). Do NOT apply the long-vol-after-debias rule to NSE single-stock options for retail: physical settlement since Oct-2019 + thin/wide spreads + flat Rs 20/order brokerage swamp the already-smaller single-name premium.

De-bias magnitude is India-sized and regime-varying: ~+1 to +1.5 vol pts in calm/normal regimes (India mean VRP ~+1.2 pts, positive ~74.9% of days), near-zero or NEGATIVE right after a VIX spike. Re-estimate on a rolling 1-2y window; do not freeze a constant.

Headwind is largest in calm/low-VIX index regimes and REVERSES in panics: once India VIX is in the upper tail (analogue of US VIX>~40-50 where the IV-RV spread goes negative), drop the haircut and favor long vol — that is the regime the framework exists to capture.

Event selectivity by event TYPE: long vol pays THROUGH genuine fat-tail events (general elections — India VIX +27.7% to 26.74 on Jun-4-2024 result day). It LOSES through anticipated binary events that crush IV (Union Budget: VIX fell all 15 of 2011-2025 budget days, avg < -9%; most RBI days; single-stock earnings). Buy only ahead of the right event type, using a weekly that expires AFTER the event resolves.

Avoid cheap far-OTM tails (premium < ~Rs 5/unit) where flat Rs 20/order brokerage is 13-30%+ round-trip and dwarfs the ~1.2pt premium; concentrate in liquid near-ATM weeklies and trade more lots per order to amortise fixed cost.

Size as a pre-budgeted insurance burn: cap monthly theta/carry bleed (Vega x expected IV-RV haircut x holding days) as a small fixed fraction of capital; keep dry powder to scale INTO the convex panic payoff. Do NOT toggle to net-short vol to fund the book (re-imports negative-skew/clustered-drawdown ruin risk).
adjudicators.resolved_tension: All three lenses converge on holds-with-caveats: the core mechanism (IV systematically > subsequent RV; long vol is negative-equity-beta "paying a premium," not alpha) is among the most robust facts in empirical finance and survived an active refutation attempt. The disagreement is not on the principle but on its DOMAIN, and the lenses agree on the four bounds, so I resolve toward recast (not core-keep) because the candidate's MECHANISM text overstates the headwind as a flat constant while the binding facts are conditional: (1) the premium is REGIME-DEPENDENT — large in calm indices, shrinking monotonically as VIX rises, and going NEGATIVE in panics (Sinclair Table 4.3); the candidate's own conflicting-claims list captures this but the body contradicts it. (2) The premium is an INDEX effect (correlation/dispersion premium); single-stock VRP is small/noisy, so the candidate's instinct that single names are "favorable" is directionally true but is a TRAP for retail once NSE physical settlement + thin liquidity + flat brokerage are layered on — net, drop single-stock long vol. (3) Magnitude is INDIA-sized (~+1.2 pt mean, smaller than US ~4 pt), so the de-bias must be ~1-1.5 pt and conditional, never US ~3.09. (4) The binding constraint the principle is silent on is EXECUTION: India VIX is untradable, QC has no NSE options data/F&O routing, so the only feasible expression is NIFTY index options via a custom-data + external-execution path. Netting out: the claim is correct and operationally useful but must be recast as a regime- and instrument-conditional de-bias rule on the NIFTY index, with a validate-on-US-then-port execution architecture — hence recast-for-nse.
citations: Sinclair, Volatility Trading 2e, Ch.4 'The Variance Premium' Table 4.3 (p.63-64): IV-RV spread 27.75% at VIX<20, narrowing monotonically and going NEGATIVE (-11.52%) at VIX>50; explicit de-bias prescription, S&P benchmark ~3.09 vol pts (p.62-65) — establishes the regime-conditional headwind and the de-bias method.

Sinclair Ch.11, Table 11.1 (p.218-219): no persistent single-stock variance premium; 'the variance premium is largely an index effect' (correlation/dispersion premium) — grounds the index-only application and single-stock exemption.

Verified fact base (NIFTY ATM IV via Black-76 vs Yang-Zhang RV, Aug-2022-Mar-2026, ~887 days): India VRP positive ~74.9% of days, mean ~+1.2 vol pts, inverts ~25.1% and going INTO shocks; India VIX AR(1) ~0.79 (half-life ~3 days), post-2014 level ~13-17; vs US VIX 19.59% / RV 15.50% ~4.1pt gap (CFA Institute) — sets the India-sized, regime-conditional haircut.

Verified fact base + QC docs: India VIX untradable (futures dead ~Feb-2016, no options/ETF/ETN); QC has NO native NSE options/F&O data; Zerodha/Samco plugins equity/commodity-only; LEAN OptionChain/Greeks/fills reject custom contracts. https://www.quantconnect.com/docs/v2/lean-cli/live-trading/brokerages/zerodha — fixes the validate-on-US-then-custom-NSE execution path.

Verified fact base on NSE structure & events: NIFTY-only weeklies, expiry moved Thursday->TUESDAY 1-Sep-2025, lot 65 (~Rs 16-17 lakh); single-stock options physically settled since Oct-2019 (DNE ~3 CTM strikes); elections pay long vol through the event (India VIX +27.7% to 26.74 on Jun-4-2024) while Budget crushes (down all 15 of 2011-2025 days). https://zerodha.com/z-connect/business-updates/revision-in-exchange-transaction-charges-and-securities-transaction-tax-from-october-1-2024

adjudicators.net_actionable: Initiate a long-vol (long-gamma) NIFTY position ONLY when your defensible realized-vol forecast over the option's life exceeds ATM IV by MORE than the structural variance premium (~+1.2 vol pts on NIFTY) PLUS round-trip cost (~0.8% ATM), AND you can name a catalyst whose OUTCOME is not consensus-priced. Treat the (1/2)S²Γ(σ_RV²−σ_IV²) edge as a noisy expectation, not a per-period drip. "IV looks cheap" is never a trade; a de-biased forecast gap plus an un-priced dated catalyst is.
conditions: Threshold de-bias: require forecast RV > IV + E[VRP] for the name/regime (~+1.2 vol pts NIFTY index; ~3-4 pts on US SPX if prototyping there), not RV > raw IV. Only the EXCESS over the premium is tradable edge.
Margin of safety vs noise: the de-biased gap must exceed both the ~25% sampling error on 30-day HV and the ~0.8% (ATM) round-trip cost. A 1-2 vol-pt gap sitting inside forecast noise is not a trade.

Named, currently-UNDERpriced catalyst is mandatory. Valid NSE long-vol catalysts = general/state elections and genuine un-priced policy/geopolitical/global shocks. EXPLICITLY EXCLUDE Union Budget (IV crush, VIX down 15/15 years, avg >9%), single-stock earnings (implied move > realized), and treat RBI as neutral-to-short.

Regime preference: favor entries when starting IV is low/compressed (cheap convexity) or when entering stress where VRP is collapsing/negative (Sinclair Table 4.3: VIX>50 premium -11.52%). Avoid new long vol in high-VRP calm grinds.

Catalyst must not be already repriced: by the time the outcome is consensus, IV has lifted (Jun-3-2024 exit-poll relief crushed VIX one day before the Jun-4 explosion). Enter before the surface prices the tail.

This is a rare-entry, lumpy-payoff strategy: size each event as a small fixed fraction (sub-Kelly), diversify across uncorrelated catalysts/dates, cap aggregate vega so a run of no-move events is survivable.
adjudicators.nse_qc_implementation: INSTRUMENT: Trade the INDEX, not single names (cash-settled = no physical-delivery trap, deeper liquidity, larger/cleaner VRP). Use NIFTY 50 weekly options — the only weekly index post-Nov-2024 (BANKNIFTY/FINNIFTY/MIDCPNIFTY are monthly-only) — expiring TUESDAY since 1-Sep-2025. EXPIRY: pick the weekly (or monthly for non-NIFTY) whose life fully spans the catalyst date; not 0DTE. STRUCTURE: for a directional-uncertain event (e.g. election result) use an ATM long straddle or slightly-OTM long strangle (1 lot = 65 units ~Rs 16-17 lakh notional; size by premium outlay, not notional). HEDGE LEG: run delta-hedged — buy options, hedge delta with NIFTY futures — so P&L is driven by RV>IV rather than direction; or cheapen entry with a calendar long the event-spanning expiry, short a non-event expiry to claw back theta. AVOID far-OTM cheap tails (flat Rs 40 brokerage + GST = 13-30% of premium round-trip). India VIX is UNTRADABLE (futures dead since ~Feb 2016, no options/ETN) so express vol only via NIFTY options. DATA+EXECUTION: QC has NO native NSE options/F&O data and NO native F&O routing (Zerodha/Samco plugins are equity/commodity only). Path A — self-source NIFTY chain+underlying minute data (TrueData/GDFL), import as custom LEAN type, compute IV/Greeks with QuantLib/py_vollib (Black-76, European index), attach custom FeeModel (Rs 20/order + STT 0.10% sell premium → 0.15% sell + 0.15% exercise from 1-Apr-2026 + NSE 0.03553%/side + SEBI 0.0001%/side + 18% GST + 0.003% stamp buy) + custom Slippage/Fill; execute live via Kite Connect API OUTSIDE LEAN with a thin signal bridge. Path B (pragmatic POC) — prototype the RV-vs-IV + catalyst methodology on QC-native US SPX/SPXW/VIX (full chains/Greeks/IV/fees from 2012), then re-implement only on self-sourced NSE data for the India edge.
adjudicators.resolved_tension: The MECHANISM (dollar-gamma × variance-gap) is verbatim Sinclair and survives the refute attempt exactly — it is not an approximation a skeptic can dislodge; the only nit is that gamma is evaluated at IV and a path-dependent term coexists, so the edge is a noisy EXPECTATION, not a smooth accrual. The counterclaims (structural VRP, ±25% HV noise) do NOT defeat the principle — they SHARPEN it: because IV is systematically biased above subsequent RV (~+1.2 vol pts India, positive ~74.9% of days; long-vol has negative unconditional returns per Bennett 3.2), the binding hurdle is forecast RV > IV + premium, not forecast RV > raw IV, and the catalyst clause is the candidate's strongest, least-refutable component (it answers "cheap vol is cheap for a reason"). The decisive NSE-specific correction: the principle's MECHANISM is exchange-agnostic and arguably more important on India's smaller-VRP market, but a catalyst list naming Budget/earnings INVERTS the sign — those are short-vol events (systematic IV crush). Net: principle holds; threshold must be de-biased; catalyst set must be restricted to genuinely un-priced events (elections, shocks); QC is unusable turnkey for NSE options so the execution form is materially recast.
citations: Sinclair Eq 7.14 (text/sinclair.txt L6328): one-step delta-hedged P&L = (1/2)(σ_i²−σ_r²)S²Γ(σ_i)dt + path-dependent noise — mechanism verified verbatim; long-gamma sign is the mirror.

Sinclair Ch.4 L3484-3592: IV structurally biased high ('forecasts 30% below implied... never the converse'), SPX spread avg 3.09 vol pts, 'subtract the usual implied/forecast spread'; Table 4.3 VRP regime-dependent (+27.75% VIX<20 → −11.52% VIX>50).
Bennett 3.1 L741 / 3.2 L3307 / L328: IV avg 1-2 pts above realized; 'long volatility strategies on average have negative returns'; 'long vol strategy should have expiry just after the key date.'

Verified fact base (India VRP): NIFTY IV > subsequent RV ~74.9% of days, mean ~+1.2 vol pts (smaller than US ~4.1), inverts ~25% of days/into shocks; India VIX mean-reverting, VRP AR(1)~0.79. Budget IV crush 15/15 years avg >9%; Jun-4-2024 election VIX +27.7% — long-vol payoff. India VIX untradable.

Verified fact base (NSE/QC): QC has no native NSE options/F&O data and no F&O routing (Zerodha/Samco equity-only); NIFTY weekly only weekly index, Tuesday expiry since 1-Sep-2025, lot ~65 ~Rs 16-17 lakh, index cash-settled vs single-stock physical delivery; STT sell 0.10%→0.15% from 1-Apr-2026. https://www.quantconnect.com/docs/v2/writing-algorithms/securities/asset-classes/india-equity/requesting-data
